name: Zoom
description: Video conferencing and collaboration platform. Yields evidence of meeting security settings (encryption, waiting rooms, recording policies), sign-in and admin activity logs, user and role reports, and recording retention configuration.

steps.action: Sign in to the Zoom web portal and navigate to Admin > Account Management > Account Settings > Meeting tab; screenshot the Security section (waiting room, passcodes, end-to-end encryption, authentication profiles)
steps.audit_note: Capture the full browser window so the account context and date are visible

steps.action: Open the Recording tab in Account Settings and screenshot cloud recording policies, including the auto-delete retention setting ('Delete cloud recordings after a specified number of days')
steps.audit_note: Evidences your recording retention policy is enforced by configuration, not just documented

steps.action: Navigate to Admin > Account Management > Reports > User Activity Reports > Sign In/Sign Out, set the date range and export the CSV
steps.permissions_required: Account Owner, Admin, or a custom role with Usage Reports permission
steps.audit_note: The portal only queries one month at a time and retains sign-in/out data for a limited period — export at least monthly to avoid gaps

steps.action: From the same Reports area, open Admin Activity Logs (formerly Operation Logs) and export the log of administrative changes (user additions, setting changes, recording deletions)
steps.permissions_required: Account Owner or a custom role with Admin Activity Logs permission
steps.audit_note: Admin activity entries are generated by Zoom and cannot be edited by administrators

steps.action: Navigate to Admin > User Management > Users and export the user list CSV; screenshot Admin > User Management > Roles to evidence role assignments
steps.audit_note: Supports periodic access review evidence — includes licence type, role and last sign-in

steps.action: Upload the screenshots and CSV exports to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Review exports for personal data before uploading and redact where required

steps.action: Navigate to Admin > Account Management > Reports and open the report subscription option (Subscription Center) to configure emailed report delivery
Add a subscription: choose the report or log type, the recipients, and a daily, weekly or monthly delivery schedule
steps.security_note: Limit recipients to authorised compliance personnel; delivery is by email, so route to a controlled mailbox
steps.audit_note: Emailed reports carry Zoom's generation timestamp — retain the delivery email with the attachment

steps.action: Note the honest limitation: subscriptions cover usage reports and Zoom Phone logs; meeting Sign In/Sign Out and Admin Activity Logs have no scheduled delivery, so keep a recurring monthly task to export those manually (or automate them at L3)
steps.audit_note: Documenting the gap and the compensating manual export preserves collection completeness

steps.action: File each delivered report into the evidence repository from the controlled mailbox, preserving the original email headers
steps.audit_note: Original headers evidence delivery time and unaltered content

steps.action: Screenshot the configured subscriptions as standing evidence that collection is scheduled rather than ad hoc
Create a Server-to-Server OAuth app in the Zoom App Marketplace (marketplace.zoom.us > Develop > Build App > Server-to-Server OAuth) and record the Account ID, Client ID and Client Secret
steps.permissions_required: Account Owner, or a role granted Server-to-Server OAuth app view/edit under User Management > Roles > Role Settings > Advanced features
steps.security_note: Store the Client Secret only in a secrets manager; it grants account-level API access without user interaction

steps.action: Add read-only scopes to the app: report:read:admin for sign-in/out activity and admin activity (operation) logs, plus user:read:admin for user listings — or their granular scope equivalents on newer apps
steps.permissions_required: Admin-level role permissions are required to add admin-level scopes
steps.audit_note: Grant only read scopes; a least-privilege app strengthens the evidentiary integrity of collected data

steps.action: Obtain access tokens with the account_credentials grant: POST https://zoom.us/oauth/token?grant_type=account_credentials&account_id={accountId} using the Client ID/Secret as HTTP Basic auth
steps.security_note: Tokens expire after one hour — cache and refresh them in the pipeline rather than logging or persisting them

steps.action: Schedule daily calls to GET https://api.zoom.us/v2/report/activities (sign in/out), GET https://api.zoom.us/v2/report/operationlogs (admin activity) and GET https://api.zoom.us/v2/users (user list), writing date-stamped output to the evidence repository
steps.permissions_required: Server-to-Server OAuth token with the scopes from step 2
steps.audit_note: Report endpoints accept a maximum one-month from/to window and cover a limited trailing period — daily collection avoids gaps

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed runs (401 token expiry, 429 rate limits, empty responses) so the evidence owner is notified
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection within your defined SLA and be recorded

steps.action: Validate each run against a baseline event volume (e.g. daily sign-in count) and flag anomalies
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Zoom Managed Evidence Pipeline with Webhooks and Metrics

steps.action: Enable Event Subscriptions on the Server-to-Server OAuth app and subscribe to relevant events (user.signed_in, user.signed_out, recording.completed, account setting change events), delivering to your HTTPS collector or SIEM endpoint
steps.permissions_required: Account Owner or admin role with Server-to-Server OAuth app edit permission
steps.security_note: Validate every delivery using the app's secret token and the x-zm-signature header; serve the endpoint over TLS 1.2+ and respond within 3 seconds

steps.action: Handle Zoom's webhook retry behaviour (three retries over ~85 minutes) with idempotent ingestion and a dead-letter queue for deliveries that still fail
steps.audit_note: Idempotent handling prevents duplicate events inflating evidence volumes

steps.action: Reconcile the webhook stream daily against pulls of GET https://api.zoom.us/v2/report/activities to detect missed deliveries and backfill gaps
steps.audit_note: API reconciliation is the completeness control for a push-based pipeline

steps.action: Track collection completeness metrics: events per day, ingest latency, webhook delivery failures and reconciliation deltas
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are key evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Build a collection-health dashboard (events/day, p95 latency, failed deliveries, backfill count) and alert when gaps exceed the defined threshold
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of process health

steps.action: Generate an automated quarterly collection-health report covering volumes, gaps, SLA breaches and credential rotation status, and file it as evidence
